//: Per-version upgrade records applied by `update` to already-installed harnesses. Keys are released versions; each entry may carry: removed (stale template paths to delete when sha-unmodified), renamed (old path -> new path), promotedModules (opt-in modules folded into the default harness), configSteps (gate steps injected into the consumer's VALIDATE_STEPS: {name, cmd, after}), configCommandUpdates (from-guarded canonical-command rewrites: {name, from, to}), seedOnInitOnly (NEW seeded exemplar paths a newer template ships as init-time-only starting content — `update` must NOT auto-plant them into an existing install; a trailing '/' matches the whole subtree, no slash matches an exact file; collected across ALL versions, not just pending, and pullable on demand via `update --refresh-seeded <path>`). Entries are applied in version order for manifest.harnessVersion < v <= installer version. See installer/lib/migrations.mjs.

0.1.6.//: packages/schema/drizzle/0002_notes_keyset_idx.sql is seedOnInitOnly for a reason that is NOT cosmetic: drizzle/ is a seeded PREFIX, so `update` would otherwise auto-plant a NEW MIGRATION into an existing project's schema — DDL they never wrote, applied to their database on the next db:migrate, with a filename that may collide with their own 0002_*. The harness does not write migrations for you. A fresh install gets the composite keyset index as part of its exemplar; an upgraded install is told to author its own by the DAL plan probe, which reds on the Sort and names the index shape (see tests/rls/plan-regression.test.ts).
